steps.title: Test the universal claim
steps.content: The student claims ego-based conceptualizations of time are universal. A finding weakens that claim most directly if it gives even one example of a culture using a time system that is not based on the speaker's orientation.

steps.title: Find the non-egocentric case
steps.content: Choice B shows a grammatical similarity to English, not a counterexample. Choice C shows a contrast between past and future but not whether it is speaker-based. Choice D concerns gesture frequency. Pointing downhill regardless of facing direction uses geography instead of ego-based orientation. The answer is A.
